segments.text: There was a relative of Naomi's husband, a man of great wealth, of the family of Elimelech. His name was Boaz.
Ruth the Moabitess said to Naomi,
Please let me go to the field, and glean heads of grain after him in whose sight I may find favor.
segments.speaker: Ruth

segments.text: And she said to her,
Go, my daughter.
segments.speaker: Naomi

segments.text: Then she left, and went and gleaned in the field after the reapers. And she happened to come to the part of the field belonging to Boaz, who was of the family of Elimelech.
Now behold, Boaz came from Bethlehem, and said to the reapers,
The Lord be with you!
segments.speaker: Boaz

segments.text: And they answered him,
The Lord bless you!
segments.speaker: Reapers

segments.text: Then Boaz said to his servant who was in charge of the reapers,
Whose young woman is this?
segments.speaker: Boaz

segments.text: So the servant who was in charge of the reapers answered and said,
It is the young Moabite woman who came back with Naomi from the country of Moab.
segments.speaker: Servant

segments.text: And she said,
Please let me glean and gather after the reapers among the sheaves.
segments.speaker: Ruth

segments.text: So she came and has continued from morning until now, though she rested a little in the house.
Then Boaz said to Ruth,
You will listen, my daughter, will you not? Do not go to glean in another field, nor go from here, but stay close by my young women.
segments.speaker: Boaz

segments.text: Let your eyes be on the field which they reap, and go after them. Have I not commanded the young men not to touch you? And when you are thirsty, go to the vessels and drink from what the young men have drawn.
segments.speaker: Boaz

segments.text: So she fell on her face, bowed down to the ground, and said to him,
Why have I found favor in your eyes, that you should take notice of me, since I am a foreigner?
segments.speaker: Ruth

segments.text: And Boaz answered and said to her,

It has been fully reported to me, all that you have done for your mother-in-law since the death of your husband, and how you have left your father and your mother and the land of your birth, and have come to a people whom you did not know before.
segments.speaker: Boaz

segments.text: The Lord repay your work, and a full reward be given you by the Lord God of Israel, under whose wings you have come for refuge.
segments.speaker: Boaz

segments.text: Then she said,
Let me find favor in your sight, my lord; for you have comforted me, and have spoken kindly to your maidservant, though I am not like one of your maidservants.
segments.speaker: Ruth

segments.text: Now Boaz said to her at mealtime,
Come here, and eat of the bread, and dip your piece of bread in the vinegar.
segments.speaker: Boaz

segments.text: So she sat beside the reapers, and he passed parched grain to her; and she ate and was satisfied, and kept some back.
And when she rose up to glean, Boaz commanded his young men, saying,
Let her glean even among the sheaves, and do not reproach her.
segments.speaker: Boaz

segments.text: Also let grain from the bundles fall purposely for her; leave it that she may glean, and do not rebuke her.
segments.speaker: Boaz

segments.text: So she gleaned in the field until evening, and beat out what she had gleaned, and it was about an ephah of barley.
Then she took it up and went into the city, and her mother-in-law saw what she had gleaned. So she brought out and gave to her what she had kept back after she had been satisfied.
And her mother-in-law said to her,
Where have you gleaned today? And where did you work? Blessed be the one who took notice of you.
segments.speaker: Naomi

segments.text: So she told her mother-in-law with whom she had worked, and said,
The man's name with whom I worked today is Boaz.
segments.speaker: Ruth

segments.text: Then Naomi said to her daughter-in-law,
Blessed be he of the Lord, who has not forsaken His kindness to the living and the dead!
segments.speaker: Naomi

segments.text: And Naomi said to her,
This man is a relation of ours, one of our close relatives.
segments.speaker: Naomi

segments.text: Ruth the Moabitess said,
He also said to me, 'You shall stay close by my young men until they have finished all my harvest.'
segments.speaker: Ruth

segments.text: And Naomi said to Ruth her daughter-in-law,
It is good, my daughter, that you go out with his young women, and that people do not meet you in any other field.
segments.speaker: Naomi

segments.text: So she stayed close by the young women of Boaz, to glean until the end of barley harvest and wheat harvest; and she dwelt with her mother-in-law.
